meaning: n.(阿尔卑斯山地农民的)少女装,紧身连衣裙
examples: Speaking of handicrafts, in addition to the dirndl.
Most women fall in love with a dirndl the moment they see one.
Despite having lived in Austria for more than a decade, I had never been tempted to wear a dirndl.
If every woman wore a dirndl, there would not be any more ugliness'.
In Mr. Drillot's relentlessly harsh lighting, Mr. Lagerfeld's dirndl-waisted skirts (think frumpy gym-class uniforms) and thin tops made both the men's and women's physiques look lumpy.
